title: WorkUnit
description: The message emitted by the specification side across the Build seam (spec §5.1). Travels BY VALUE: the SPMC bundle is inlined in full, with every reference resolved, so the executor resolves nothing by calling back. The universal envelope below is what every conforming executor must understand; stack-specific structure (capability tiers, escalation ladders, sealed interior DAGs, etc.) lives in `executor_extension`, which the framework neither reads nor mandates.

unit_ref.description: Opaque identifier for this unit. The executor treats it as a label, NOT a pointer into the specification side's graph. Echoed back on the verdict so a consumer can match verdict to unit without shared state.

parent_lineage.description: Opaque lineage tag carried solely so a verdict can be rolled up to the larger deliverable it belongs to. Opaque to the executor; echoed back on the verdict. Optional: a unit with no larger parent may omit it.

bundle_hash.description: Content hash of `bundle` as emitted. This IS the unit's identity. Present even though the bundle travels by value, because the verdict needs it and because it makes a future migration to by-reference transport additive, not breaking (move the body to a content-addressed store, keep the hash). The emitter MUST ensure this equals the hash of `bundle` at emit time.

bundle.description: The frozen SPMC bundle (spec §5) — the Input Contract, fully self-contained. The four axes are named in this framework's terms; an executor reads `schema`, `prompt`, and `context` to produce the artifact, and matches `model` to a capability it can supply. No element may require a callback to read: if the executor would ever need to resolve a reference live, the unit is not frozen and MUST NOT be emitted.

schema.description: The shape of the output the unit must produce: the artifact's type and its versioned acceptance criteria. What 'one artifact' is checked against (spec §5, §6.1).

prompt.description: The single bounded transformation the unit performs — the unit's 'single purpose' (spec §5). Free-form to the framework; meaningful to the executor's producer.

model.description: What should produce the artifact: the capability the unit is intended for, declared abstractly. The executor matches this to a resident producer (spec §5.1). The framework does not enumerate capability values — an executor's catalog is its own concern; the framework only requires that the unit declares the capability it needs.

context.description: The frozen, resolved input the unit reads: every What concept and How principle it depends on, referenced by pointer and resolved/inlined. The axis the reproducibility guarantee rests on — freeze the context, and the same input yields the same output (spec §5, §5.1).

acceptance_class.description: Tells a CONSUMER whether an `accepted` verdict may auto-commit without human review (`auto-commit-if-green`) or must be surfaced for a human (`needs-verdict`). The executor passes it through unchanged; it does not interpret deliverable-level meaning. A consumer-facing, framework-level field — universal because every executor must carry it even though none acts on it.

executor_extension.description: THE ONE OPAQUE SLOT. Stack-specific structure a particular execution framework needs — capability tiers, escalation-ladder position, sealed interior dependency graphs, batching hints, and so on — lives here. The framework NEITHER reads NOR mandates anything inside it. Its presence means an executor never needs the universal schema reopened to carry its own model. A `kind` discriminator is recommended so a consumer can tell which executor produced it, but the contents are otherwise unconstrained.

kind.description: Optional discriminator naming the execution framework whose structure this slot carries (e.g. a reverse-DNS or short name). Lets a consumer recognise the extension's shape without the framework defining it.
